product.one_line_description: Stripe provides a RESTful API for processing payments, managing customers, handling subscriptions, and other financial operations.

authentication.auth_summary: The Stripe API uses API keys to authenticate requests. Test mode secret keys start with 'sk_test_' and provide unrestricted access to sandboxes. Live mode uses restricted API keys (starts with 'rk_live_') with specific permissions for security. All API requests must be made over HTTPS and without authentication, they will fail.

developer_access.access_notes: Developers can view and manage their API keys in the Stripe Dashboard. Test mode secret keys are available for sandboxes, and restricted API keys can be created for live mode with specific permissions. These keys are obtainable without human approval.

api_surface.api_summary: The Stripe API is a public, REST-based API organized around predictable resource-oriented URLs. It accepts form-encoded request bodies, returns JSON-encoded responses, and uses standard HTTP response codes and verbs. It supports client libraries in Ruby, Python, PHP, Java, Node.js, Go, and .NET. The API provides extensive resources for core financial operations (e.g., Accounts, Charges, Customers, Payment Intents, Refunds), payment methods (e.g., Cards, Bank Accounts), products and pricing, billing (e.g., Invoices, Subscriptions), and Connect for platform businesses. It also supports webhooks for event notifications.

mcp.mcp_summary: No information about Model Context Protocol server availability was found in the provided source.

buildability.verdict_reasoning: Critical atomic facts regarding public API availability or credential gating remain unclear.

evidence_pool.claim: Stripe provides a RESTful API for processing payments, managing customers, handling subscriptions, and other financial operations.
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
The Stripe API is organized around [REST]. Our API has predictable resource-oriented URLs, accepts [form-encoded] request bodies, returns [JSON-encoded] responses, and uses standard HTTP response codes, authentication, and verbs. 
Core Resources
[Charges](https://docs.stripe.com/api/charges)
[Customers](https://docs.stripe.com/api/customers)
[Payment Intents](https://docs.stripe.com/api/payment_intents)
[Refunds](https://docs.stripe.com/api/refunds)
Billing
[Subscriptions](https://docs.stripe.com/api/subscriptions)
[Invoices](https://docs.stripe.com/api/invoices)

The Stripe API uses [API keys] to authenticate requests. You can view and manage your API keys in [the Stripe Dashboard].
Test mode secret keys start with `sk_test_` and have unrestricted access to their sandboxes. In live mode, you configure a [restricted API key] (starts with `rk_live_`) with specific API permissions.

evidence_pool.claim: The Stripe API uses API keys to authenticate requests. Test mode secret keys start with 'sk_test_' and provide unrestricted access to sandboxes. Live mode uses restricted API keys (starts with 'rk_live_') with specific permissions for security. All API requests must be made over HTTPS and without authentication, they will fail.
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
The Stripe API uses [API keys] to authenticate requests. You can view and manage your API keys in [the Stripe Dashboard].
Test mode secret keys start with `sk_test_` and have unrestricted access to their sandboxes. In live mode, you configure a [restricted API key] (starts with `rk_live_`) with specific API permissions.
Make all API requests over [HTTPS]. Calls made over plain HTTP fail. API requests without authentication also fail.

You can view and manage your API keys in [the Stripe Dashboard].
Test mode secret keys start with `sk_test_` and have unrestricted access to their sandboxes. In both test mode and live mode, you can create as many restricted API keys as you need for different use cases or components of your application.

You can view and manage your API keys in [the Stripe Dashboard].
Test mode secret keys start with `sk_test_` and have unrestricted access to their sandboxes. In both test mode and live mode, you can create as many restricted API keys as you need for different use cases or components of your application.

evidence_pool.claim: Developers can view and manage their API keys in the Stripe Dashboard. Test mode secret keys are available for sandboxes, and restricted API keys can be created for live mode with specific permissions. These keys are obtainable without human approval.
evidence_pool.evidence_snippet:
The Stripe API uses [API keys] to authenticate requests. You can view and manage your API keys in [the Stripe Dashboard].
Test mode secret keys start with `sk_test_` and have unrestricted access to their sandboxes. In live mode, you configure a [restricted API key] (starts with `rk_live_`) with specific API permissions. Using a restricted API key with only a subset of API permissions limits the damage a bad actor could cause if they obtained the key. In both test mode and live mode, you can create as many restricted API keys as you need for different use cases or components of your application.
